question: Who is the author of the story 'The Enemy'?
explanation: The story 'The Enemy' is written by Pearl S. Buck.
subject: English

question: Where was Dr. Sadao Hoki's house built?
options: In the middle of a bustling city
On a spot of the Japanese coast
explanation: Dr Sadao Hoki’s house was built on a spot of the Japanese coast where as a little boy he had often played.
subject: English

question: What did Sadao's father call the islands of the South Seas?
options: The stepping stones to the future for Japan
explanation: His father said, "Those islands yonder, they are the stepping stones to the future for Japan."
subject: English

question: What was the chief concern of Sadao's father?
explanation: Sadao knew that his education was his father’s chief concern.
subject: English

question: At what age was Sadao sent to America to learn surgery and medicine?
explanation: He had been sent at twenty-two to America to learn all that could be learned of surgery and medicine.
subject: English

question: At what age did Sadao return from America?
explanation: He had come back at thirty, and before his father died he had seen Sadao become famous.
subject: English

question: Why was Sadao not sent abroad with the troops?
options: He was too old to fight
He refused to fight for his country
He was perfecting a discovery to render wounds entirely clean
He was injured in a previous battle
explanation: Because he was perfecting a discovery which would render wounds entirely clean, he had not been sent abroad with the troops. Also, the General might need an operation.
subject: English

question: Where did Sadao first meet Hana?
explanation: He had found her in the most casual way, by chance literally, at an American professor’s house (Professor Harley's house).
subject: English

question: Why did Sadao wait to fall in love with Hana in America?
options: He was focused entirely on his studies
He wanted to be sure she was Japanese
He was waiting for his father's permission
He did not like her initially
explanation: He had waited to fall in love with her until he was sure she was Japanese, because his father would never have received her unless she had been pure in her race.
subject: English

question: How was Sadao and Hana's marriage arranged?
options: In the old Japanese way after his father saw her
explanation: When his father had seen her the marriage had been arranged in the old Japanese way.
subject: English

question: What did Sadao and Hana see coming out of the mists while standing on the veranda?
options: A black figure of a man
explanation: It was at this moment that both of them saw something black come out of the mists. It was a man.
subject: English

question: What did Sadao initially think the man was when he saw him staggering?
options: A fisherman washed from his boat
explanation: "A fisherman perhaps," Sadao said, "washed from his boat."
subject: English

question: What did Sadao initially use to pack the man's bleeding wound on the beach?
explanation: He packed the wound with the sea moss that strewed the beach.
subject: English

question: What lettering was faintly visible on the battered cap of the wounded man?
explanation: He spelled it out: "U.S. Navy." The man was a prisoner of war!
subject: English

question: What did Sadao and Hana initially think would be the kindest thing to do with the man?
options: To put him back into the sea
To hide him in the basement
To turn him over to the police immediately
explanation: Sadao said, "The best thing that we could do would be to put him back in the sea," and Hana agreed.
subject: English

question: Why did Sadao say he cared nothing for the man if he were whole?
options: Because he is a common fellow and all Americans are his enemy
Because Sadao was a very arrogant doctor
Because the man had insulted him
explanation: "I care nothing for him. He is my enemy. All Americans are my enemy. And he is only a common fellow."
subject: English

question: Whose empty bedroom did they carry the wounded man into?
explanation: It had been the bedroom of Sadao’s father, and since his death it had not been used.
subject: English

question: Why was everything in Sadao's father's room Japanese?
options: To please the old man, who would never sit on a chair or sleep in a foreign bed
Because it was the cheapest furniture available
explanation: Everything here had been Japanese to please the old man, who would never in his own home sit on a chair or sleep in a foreign bed.
subject: English

question: Who did Hana initially say should wash the wounded man?
explanation: Hana said, "I will tell Yumi now. She can leave the children for a few minutes and she can wash him."
subject: English

question: What did the old gardener say about the white man's wounds?
options: That if the master heals what the gun and the sea did, they will take revenge on them
That he should be given to the police instantly
That the sea moss would cure him
explanation: The gardener said, "If the master heals what the gun did and what the sea did they will take revenge on us."
subject: English

question: What was Yumi's reaction when told to wash the white man?
options: She ran out of the house crying
She refused, saying she had never washed a white man and would not wash a dirty one
She demanded more pay to do it
explanation: "I have never washed a white man," she said, "and I will not wash so dirty a one now."
subject: English

question: Who ended up washing the upper body of the white man?
explanation: Hana dipped the small clean towel into the steaming hot water and washed his face carefully... she kept on washing him until his upper body was quite clean.
subject: English

question: Where did Hana get the strips of matting to try and protect the floor from blood?
options: From the back veranda where the gardener kept them
explanation: She went out to the back veranda where the gardener kept strips of matting with which to protect delicate shrubs on cold nights.
subject: English

question: What task did Sadao assign to Hana during the operation?
options: To give the anesthetic if the man needs it
explanation: "You will have to give the anesthetic if he needs it," he said.
subject: English

question: What happened to Hana when the blood began to flow more quickly during the operation?
options: She choked, clapped her hands to her mouth, and ran out of the room to retch
She screamed at Sadao to stop
explanation: At this moment Hana choked... She clapped her hands to her mouth and leaped up and ran out of the room. Outside in the garden he heard her retching.
subject: English

question: Why did Sadao feel impatient and irritable with the man while operating?
options: Because the man was an American
Because the operation was taking too long
Because of Hana's distress and his inability to go to her at once
Because he lacked the proper instruments
explanation: But her distress and his inability to go to her at once made him impatient and irritable with this man who lay like dead under his knife.
subject: English

question: What did Hana observe on the young man's neck while administering the anesthetic?
options: A tattoo of the American flag
Deep red scars just under the ear
A necklace with a dog tag
explanation: It was at this moment that she observed deep red scars on his neck, just under the ear.
subject: English

question: According to Sadao's old American professor of anatomy, what is the surgeon's cardinal sin?
explanation: "Ignorance of the human body is the surgeon’s cardinal sin, sirs!" he had thundered at his classes.
subject: English

question: What phrase did Sadao habitually murmur to his patients when he forgot himself in an operation?
explanation: "My friend," he always called his patients and so now he did, forgetting that this was his enemy.
subject: English

question: What were the few English words the man muttered unconsciously after the bullet was removed?
explanation: "Guts," he muttered, choking. "They got...my guts..."
subject: English

question: How old did Sadao estimate the young man to be when he sat up on the third day?
explanation: He looked just now barely seventeen.
subject: English

question: What did the cook do to demonstrate her contempt for Sadao's decision to save the enemy?
options: She split a fowl's neck and let its blood flow into a wistaria vine
She refused to cook any more meals
explanation: She split a fowl’s neck skillfully and held the fluttering bird and let its blood flow into the roots of a wistaria vine.
subject: English

question: What was the American prisoner's name?
explanation: He had said in his impulsive way, "Anyway, let me tell you that my name is Tom."
subject: English

question: Sadao typed a letter reporting the whole matter. Who was it addressed to?
explanation: Sadao went back to his office and carefully typed a letter to the Chief of police reporting the whole matter.
subject: English

question: On which day did the servants tie their belongings in kerchiefs and leave together?
explanation: On the seventh day after that, two things happened. In the morning the servants left together...
subject: English

question: Why was Yumi crying when she left the house?
options: She was afraid of the police
explanation: Yumi cried because of the children.
subject: English

question: Why did Hana's hands go weak when a messenger in official uniform arrived?
options: She thought the messenger was an American soldier
She thought the servants had reported them and he was there to arrest Sadao
She had been working too hard
She recognized him as a family enemy
explanation: Her hands went weak and she could not draw her breath. The servants must have told already. She ran to Sadao, gasping... "I thought they had come to arrest you", she said.
subject: English

question: What was the actual reason the messenger in official uniform came to Sadao's house?
options: To bring a letter from Sadao's father
To summon Sadao because the old General was in pain again
explanation: "You are to come to the palace," the man said. "The old General is in pain again."
subject: English

question: What plan did the General propose to get rid of the American prisoner?
options: Hand him over to the military police
Send two private assassins to kill him quietly and remove the body
Put him on a boat back to America
explanation: The General said, "Suppose I send two of them [private assassins] to your house tonight... they make no noise and they know the trick of inward bleeding. If you like I can even have them remove the body."
subject: English

question: What did Sadao have to do to facilitate the General's plan for the assassins?
options: Leave the outer partition of the white man's room open to the garden while he sleeps
Give the assassins a spare key
Dru the American so he wouldn't wake up
explanation: The General suggested, "what would be more natural than that you should leave the outer partition of the white man’s room open to the garden while he sleeps?"
subject: English

question: How did Sadao sleep the night after making the agreement with the General?
options: He slept badly, waking up thinking he heard footsteps or noises in the garden
He did not sleep at all, standing guard outside the room
He slept deeply because he was relieved
explanation: Sadao slept badly that night. Time and time again he woke, thinking he heard the rustling of footsteps...
subject: English

question: What did Sadao find in the guest room on the morning after the third night of waiting for the assassins?
options: The young man, gay, washed, and on his feet
explanation: Yet when he opened the door of the guest room in the morning there was the young man. He was very gay and had already washed and was now on his feet.
subject: English

question: What did Sadao offer the American to help him escape?
options: His stout boat with food, water, and quilts
A disguise as a Japanese soldier
explanation: Sadao says, "I think if I put my boat on the shore tonight, with food and extra clothing in it..."
subject: English

question: Where was the American supposed to row the boat to wait for rescue?
options: A little island not far from the coast
explanation: Sadao told him, "you might be able to row to that little island not far from the coast."
subject: English

question: What type of boat was the American supposed to wait for at the island?
explanation: Sadao told him, "You could live there until you saw a Korean fishing boat pass by."
subject: English

question: What was the American supposed to do if his food ran out before he caught a boat?
options: Signal one flash with his flashlight at midnight
Signal two flashes at the same instant the sun drops over the horizon
explanation: "If your food runs out before you catch a boat," he said, "signal me two flashes at the same instant the sun drops over the horizon."
subject: English

question: What item did Sadao give the American to wrap around his blond head at the last moment?
explanation: ...at the last moment Sadao wrapped a black cloth about his blond head.
subject: English

question: What was the General's excuse for not sending the assassins?
options: He changed his mind and wanted to spare the boy
He could not find them in time
He was suffering a good deal and thought of nothing but himself, forgetting his promise
He wanted Sadao to do it himself
explanation: The General said, "But you see, I was suffering a good deal. The truth is, I thought of nothing but myself. In short, I forgot my promise to you."
subject: English

question: Why did Sadao despise his old landlady in America, even though she had nursed him through influenza?
options: She was ignorant, dirty, and repulsive to him in her kindness
She tried to turn him into the police
explanation: How he had despised the ignorant and dirty old woman who had at last consented to house him... it was difficult [to be grateful], for she was no less repulsive to him in her kindness.
subject: English

question: What is Sadao's final thought as he gazes out to the sea at the end of the story?
options: He wonders if he will be arrested
He wonders if the American survived
He wonders why he could not kill the American
He wonders when the war will end
explanation: "Strange," he thought. "I wonder why I could not kill him?"
subject: English
